description: Self-clustering at different corpus sizes (sentences subsampled from 100K Leipzig corpora)
methodology_note: Earlier analysis used 10K-sentence corpora which produced inflated SC for Uralic languages. 100K corpora give more stable estimates.
conclusions: Arabic prefix SC is stable across corpus sizes (1.50-1.64x), but Arabic is SUFFIX-DOMINANT under bidirectional analysis (prefix 1.92x, suffix 2.66x, ratio 0.72). The earlier 'Arabic match' claim was retired.
Finnish and Estonian SC fluctuate around 0.85-0.97x. Never clearly positive. Earlier values (1.16x, 1.35x) were from 10K corpora and are not reproducible at larger sizes.
The Uralic match reported in earlier analysis was a false positive from small corpus size.
Voynich SC is stable at ~1.43x regardless of subsample size.
Under bidirectional analysis, no tested language matches Voynich's symmetric-high profile.
